description_long: This is a large, weathered stone vase with a rounded, bulbous body and a small, slightly domed lid featuring a knob at the top. The surface is mottled with patches of white and greenish discoloration, likely from lichen or moss, indicating prolonged outdoor exposure. The lower half of the vase is adorned with vertical fluting, giving it a classical, ornamental appearance. The back of the vase is open, revealing a hollow interior, which suggests it may have once served as a decorative garden urn or planter. The stone material appears rough and aged, with visible surface irregularities and some erosion. Overall, the vase exudes an antique, stately presence suitable for parks or historical urban settings.
description: A large, weathered stone vase with a domed lid, vertical fluting, and a mottled, aged surface. The back is open, revealing a hollow interior.
description_view_0: Front view shows the rounded body, domed lid with knob, and vertical fluting; surface is mottled and aged.
description_view_1: Side view highlights the curvature, fluting, and greenish discoloration on the upper section.
description_view_2: Rear view reveals a large open section, exposing the hollow interior of the vase.
description_view_3: Opposite side view displays more surface weathering and the continuation of the fluted design.
